title: gh-126595: fix a crash when calling `itertools.count(sys.maxsize)`
author: picnixz

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body:
> But I doubt this should be backported.

Considering it's a bug that makes the interpreter crash, I think this should.

comments.author: skirpichev
comments.body:
> Considering it's a bug that makes the interpreter crash

Only for debug builds.

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body: Yes, but I still think it's better to backport it since it can be used by third-party libraries (let's discuss it on the issue instead).

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body: Ok, maybe it's better to have a separate PR actually.

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body: I'm reverting the `__repr__` check and delegate it to https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/126620. The rationale is that there are some free-threaded update to do and I want to ease backports.

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body: I think it will be more likely to have cnt equal to maxsize rather than having an error. Which is why I put that test first.

comments.author: skirpichev
comments.body:
> I think it will be more likely to have cnt equal to maxsize rather than having an error.

Well, in terms of probability - it's more likely to have an error (this happens on countable subset of integers), rather having cnt equal to some fixed value ;)

comments.author: picnixz
comments.body: Actually I was wrong. I thought that the error only happened in bad situations but the overflow exception happens when you put something > maxsize so *your* fix is better!

comments.author: miss-islington-app[bot]
comments.body:
Thanks @picnixz for the PR, and @vstinner for merging it 🌮🎉.. I'm working now to backport this PR to: 3.12, 3.13.
🐍🍒⛏🤖

comments.author: bedevere-app[bot]
comments.body: [GH-126739](https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/126739) is a backport of this pull request to the [3.13 branch](https://github.com/python/cpython/tree/3.13).

comments.author: miss-islington-app[bot]
comments.body:
Thanks @picnixz for the PR, and @vstinner for merging it 🌮🎉.. I'm working now to backport this PR to: 3.12.
🐍🍒⛏🤖

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: I don't know what is going on with the 3.12 backport, it seems to be stuck.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: Merged, thanks @picnixz for the fix and thanks @devdanzin for the bug report.
